headline: Transcript: Should banks foot the bill for cyber scams?
summary: Persis Love talks to Joshua Franklin and Harry Dempsey
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the FT News Briefing podcast episode: ‘Should banks foot the bill for cyber scams?’

Persis LoveGood morning from the Financial Times. Today is Monday, December 30th and this is your FT News Briefing. US credit card defaults have hit the highest level since 2010, and online scams are soaring. But who should foot the bill? Plus, Japanese manufacturing conglomerate Hitachi has rethought its business model and it’s paying off. I’m Persis love. And here’s the news you need to start your day.

[MUSIC PLAYING]

US consumers are struggling to pay off their credit card debts. Defaults on loans haven’t been this high since the aftermath of the 2008 financial crash. For the first three quarters of this year, the amount that lenders write off in unpaid loans was up 50 per cent on the same period last year. Low-income households have been hit badly by years of high inflation and expensive borrowing costs. And experts worry that Donald Trump’s threat of tariffs could
